studyName: Molecular Epidemiology of Colorectal Cancer (MECC) Metastasis Study

tables.description: Sample ID, analyte type, body site where from sample was obtained, histological type of sample, and tumor status of sample obtained from participants with or without colorectal cancer and involved in the "Molecular Epidemiology of Colorectal Cancer (MECC) Metastasis Study: A Germline Variant on Chromosome 4q31.1 Associates with Susceptibility to Developing Colon Cancer Metastasis" project.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: HISTOLOGICAL_TYPE
variables.description: Cell or tissue type or subtype of sample

tables.description: Subject ID, sex, affection status, age at diagnosis, and colorectal cancer stage category of participants with or without colorectal cancer and involved in the "Molecular Epidemiology of Colorectal Cancer (MECC) Metastasis Study: A Germline Variant on Chromosome 4q31.1 Associates with Susceptibility to Developing Colon Cancer Metastasis" project.

variables.name: AGE
variables.description: Age at diagnosis for cases, age at recruitment for controls

variables.name: STAGER
variables.description: Colorectal cancer stage category at the time of diagnosis for cases

tables.description: Subject ID and consent group of participants with or without colorectal cancer and involved in the "Molecular Epidemiology of Colorectal Cancer (MECC) Metastasis Study: A Germline Variant on Chromosome 4q31.1 Associates with Susceptibility to Developing Colon Cancer Metastasis" project.

variables.name: AFFECTION_STATUS
variables.description: Case control status of the subject

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

tables.description: Subject ID and sample ID of participants with or without colorectal cancer and involved in the "Molecular Epidemiology of Colorectal Cancer (MECC) Metastasis Study: A Germline Variant on Chromosome 4q31.1 Associates with Susceptibility to Developing Colon Cancer Metastasis" project.
